entries.title: U.A. Sports Festival Is A Public Recruitment Stage

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Sports Festival turns first-year performance into public reputation, internship attention, and rival-course pressure.
content.injection: Sports Festival scenes should feel public: agencies, media, families, rival courses, and future internships all watch.
constraints: The festival is school activity, recruitment stage, and public spectacle at once.
antiLore: Do not treat the Sports Festival as a private class tournament.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Post-USJ Security Makes The Festival Defiant

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: After USJ, U.A. continues with the Sports Festival while increasing security, turning the event into a public assertion that the school is not cowed by villain attack.
content.injection: After USJ, U.A. should feel security-conscious but determined to project confidence through the Sports Festival.
constraints: Security can be mentioned without making the event lockdown-like.
antiLore: Do not ignore the institutional pressure created by USJ.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Class 1-A Carries A Post-USJ Reputation

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Class 1-A enters the Sports Festival with unusual attention because they survived a real villain attack at USJ.
content.injection: During the Sports Festival, other students can view Class 1-A as lucky, overpraised, dangerous, or legitimate competition because of USJ.
constraints: The reputation affects peer rivalry more than formal ranking.
antiLore: Do not make all other students admire Class 1-A uncritically.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Other Courses Resent Hero Course Visibility

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Students outside Class 1-A may resent how much visibility and opportunity the Hero Course receives.
content.injection: Sports Festival scenes should allow rival courses to push back against Class 1-A attention, especially General Studies students who want a path into Heroics.
constraints: Rivalry can be ambitious and institutional, not only petty.
antiLore: Do not reduce non-Hero Course students to background spectators.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Bakugo Declares First Place Without Diplomacy

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Bakugo declares he will take first place in a way that angers other students but reflects sincere confidence rather than showmanship.
content.injection: When Bakugo speaks at the Sports Festival, keep his arrogance blunt and alienating, but grounded in real competitive seriousness.
constraints: His declaration increases pressure on Class 1-A.
antiLore: Do not portray Bakugo as making a friendly motivational speech.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Todoroki Targets Izuku Before The Festival

context.label: Todoroki challenges Izuku before the festival

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shoto Todoroki challenges Izuku partly because he suspects a special connection between Izuku and All Might.
content.injection: Before their match, Shoto should treat Izuku as a serious rival tied to All Might, not merely as another classmate.
constraints: Shoto does not know One For All.
antiLore: Do not let Shoto correctly identify One For All.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Obstacle Race Rewards Adaptation Over Raw Power

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The obstacle race tests movement, situational judgment, and adaptation, letting Izuku compete through analysis rather than safe One For All control.
content.injection: In the obstacle race, emphasize improvised tactics and battlefield reading. Izuku should avoid casually using One For All as if it is already controlled.
constraints: Izuku can win without proving superior baseline power.
antiLore: Do not portray Izuku as safely mobile with Full Cowling yet.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Izuku Uses Scraps And Mines To Win The Race

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Izuku wins the obstacle race by using debris and land mines to launch himself ahead rather than by brute-force mastery of One For All.
content.injection: After the obstacle race, Izuku should be recognized for tactical improvisation, not because he has already mastered his Quirk.
constraints: This makes him a massive target in the cavalry battle.
antiLore: Do not treat the race win as proof Izuku can freely control One For All.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Ten Million Points Makes Izuku The Target

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Because Izuku places first in the obstacle race, the cavalry battle assigns him a ten-million-point headband that makes him the main target.
content.injection: During the cavalry battle, Izuku should feel hunted by nearly everyone because his headband can overturn the standings.
constraints: Alliances and tactics matter more than simple combat.
antiLore: Do not let Izuku move through the cavalry battle unnoticed.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Team Midoriya Is A Tactical Improvisation Group

context.label: Team Midoriya forms for cavalry battle

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Team Midoriya survives the cavalry battle through a mix of Ochaco’s mobility, Tokoyami’s defense, Mei’s self-promotion, and Izuku’s planning.
content.injection: When Team Midoriya is active, show uneven motives and complementary abilities rather than a polished combat unit.
constraints: Mei’s support-course agenda is openly self-promotional.
antiLore: Do not make the team look like a trained permanent squad.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Mei Uses The Festival To Advertise Support Gear

context.label: Team Midoriya forms for cavalry battle

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mei Hatsume treats the Sports Festival as an advertising platform for her inventions and support gear.
content.injection: Mei should prioritize showing off her “babies” to companies and viewers, even when cooperating with hero-course students.
constraints: Her help can be real while still self-serving.
antiLore: Do not portray Mei as motivated mainly by tournament glory.

entries.title: Shinso Brainwashing Requires A Verbal Response

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hitoshi Shinso’s Brainwashing activates when a target verbally responds to him, making provocation and silence tactically important.
content.injection: When Shinso is involved, treat speech as a tactical hazard. Characters who understand his Quirk should avoid answering him directly.
constraints: At this point Shinso lacks hero-course combat training but has a dangerous capture condition.
antiLore: Do not let Brainwashing trigger without a response.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: Brainwashing
constraints: Requires the target to verbally respond to Shinso.
Physical shock or outside interference can break control.
antiLore: Do not make Brainwashing a passive aura or eye-contact power.

entries.title: Shinso Exposes Hero Course Gatekeeping

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shinso resents being outside the Hero Course despite having a Quirk suited to capture work and heroics.
content.injection: Shinso scenes should carry institutional frustration: he wants recognition, not pity, and he tests Hero Course privilege directly.
constraints: His resentment does not make him a villain.
antiLore: Do not frame Shinso’s Quirk as inherently villainous.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Vestige Interference Saves Izuku Against Shinso

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Izuku breaks Shinso’s Brainwashing through an early, poorly understood One For All vestige-like intervention.
content.injection: In the Shinso match, Izuku must not fully understand why he escaped Brainwashing. Treat the moment as a mysterious One For All anomaly, not as mastered vestige communication.
constraints: This is a future-seeded clue, not a fully explained power system yet.
antiLore: Do not reveal the full vestige system during the Sports Festival.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Ochaco Chooses A Direct Fight With Bakugo

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Ochaco fights Bakugo with her own plan and resolve rather than relying on Izuku’s protection or being treated as helpless.
content.injection: In Ochaco vs. Bakugo scenes, preserve Ochaco’s agency. Her strategy can fail without making her weak or decorative.
constraints: Bakugo takes her seriously as an opponent.
antiLore: Do not frame Bakugo holding back because Ochaco is a girl.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Bakugo Takes Ochaco Seriously

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Bakugo treats Ochaco as dangerous enough to fight seriously, which is harsh but not dismissive.
content.injection: When Bakugo faces Ochaco, his refusal to go easy should read as combat respect through his abrasive worldview.
constraints: His language can still be crude and aggressive.
antiLore: Do not make Bakugo secretly gentle or patronizing in this match.

entries.title: Todoroki Rejects His Fire Because Of Endeavor

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shoto refuses to use his left-side fire because he associates it with Endeavor’s abuse and his family’s trauma.
content.injection: Before and during the Izuku match, Shoto’s fire refusal should be emotional and ideological, not a simple tactical preference.
constraints: His ice side is powerful but emotionally tied to rejection of Endeavor.
antiLore: Do not portray Shoto’s fire avoidance as laziness or lack of ability.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Endeavor Views Shoto As A Project

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Endeavor treats Shoto as the product of his ambition to surpass All Might, placing heavy pressure on Shoto’s public performance.
content.injection: During the Sports Festival, Endeavor should be intense, controlling, and fixated on Shoto’s usefulness as proof of his own ambition.
constraints: Endeavor is not yet in his later atonement stage.
antiLore: Do not soften Endeavor into a supportive parent in this arc.

entries.title: Izuku Pushes Shoto To Claim His Own Power

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Izuku pushes Shoto to see his fire as his own power rather than Endeavor’s possession.
content.injection: In Izuku vs. Shoto, make Izuku’s provocation moral and empathetic: he wants Shoto to fight fully as himself, even at personal cost.
constraints: This is a relationship pivot, not just a battle tactic.
antiLore: Do not make Izuku’s goal simply tournament victory.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Todoroki Uses Fire Publicly Against Izuku

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shoto uses his fire publicly against Izuku, marking a breakthrough in his relationship to his own power.
content.injection: After the Izuku match, Shoto’s use of fire is possible but emotionally complicated; do not make him instantly healed or fully integrated.
constraints: The breakthrough opens a path, not a completed recovery.
antiLore: Do not make Shoto casually spam fire with no emotional residue immediately after.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Izuku Injures Himself To Force Full Effort

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Izuku still uses One For All in body-damaging bursts during the Sports Festival, repeatedly injuring himself to push Shoto.
content.injection: Sports Festival Izuku can make spectacular attacks, but each One For All burst should carry injury cost and tactical limitation.
constraints: He has not developed Full Cowling yet.
antiLore: Do not portray him as using Full Cowling during the Sports Festival.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: One For All
constraints: Burst use causes severe self-injury at this stage.
Izuku must ration fingers and limbs as tactical resources.
antiLore: Do not give Izuku safe continuous One For All control before Gran Torino.

entries.title: Bakugo Wins But Feels Denied A True Victory

context.label: Bakugo faces Todoroki in the final

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Bakugo wins the Sports Festival final, but Todoroki’s refusal to use fire makes the victory feel hollow to him.
content.injection: After the final, Bakugo should be furious because winning without full resistance does not satisfy his competitive standard.
constraints: He wants to prove superiority against opponents at full strength.
antiLore: Do not portray the gold medal as emotionally satisfying for Bakugo.

content.fact: Sports Festival performance directly affects which students receive attention and workplace training offers from pro heroes.
content.injection: After the Sports Festival, internship opportunities should reflect public performance, Quirk visibility, and pro-hero scouting interests.
constraints: High visibility can help or burden students.
antiLore: Do not make internship offers unrelated to Sports Festival exposure.

entries.title: Todoroki Visits His Mother After The Festival

content.fact: After the Sports Festival, Shoto decides to visit his mother, beginning a difficult personal step rather than resolving the family trauma instantly.
content.injection: Post-Sports Festival Shoto may seek contact with his mother, but keep the family wound active and unresolved.
constraints: This is not Endeavor’s redemption arc.
antiLore: Do not make the Todoroki family healed after one match.

entries.title: Iida Loses Focus After Tensei Is Hurt

content.fact: After Tensei is attacked by Stain, Tenya’s usual discipline is destabilized by grief and anger.
content.injection: After Tensei’s injury, Iida should seem outwardly controlled but internally pulled toward revenge and the Ingenium legacy.
constraints: This emotional state leads into Hosu.
antiLore: Do not keep Iida purely rule-bound and emotionally untouched after Tensei’s injury.

content.fact: Neito Monoma often channels Class 1-B pride through theatrical provocation toward Class 1-A.
content.injection: Use Monoma as a rival-course irritant whose barbs reflect Class 1-B resentment and ambition, not just random malice.
antiLore: Do not portray all Class 1-B rivalry through Monoma alone.

content.fact: For General Studies students like Shinso, the Sports Festival can be a rare path toward recognition and possible Hero Course transfer.
content.injection: General Studies ambition should feel structurally motivated: the festival is one of the few public chances to be seen.
antiLore: Do not treat Shinso’s ambition as merely personal jealousy.

entries.title: Sports Festival Does Not Reveal One For All Publicly

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Sports Festival shows Izuku’s strange power but does not publicly reveal One For All’s transfer lineage.
content.injection: Audience members may notice Izuku’s power resembles All Might’s, but they must not know the One For All secret from the festival alone.
constraints: Todoroki suspects a special connection, not the full mechanism.
antiLore: Do not let classmates or media casually identify One For All.

entries.title: Recovery Girl Warns Against Repeated Self-Destruction

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Izuku’s repeated One For All injuries create real medical concern and cannot be healed indefinitely without consequences.
content.injection: After major Sports Festival injuries, Recovery Girl and responsible adults should treat Izuku’s self-destruction as a serious problem.
antiLore: Do not make healing remove all cost from repeated limb destruction.

entries.title: Sports Festival Outcomes Should Not Grant Pro Licenses

content.fact: Sports Festival success brings attention and offers, not provisional licenses or independent authority to act as pro heroes.
content.injection: After the Sports Festival, students remain unlicensed first-years; internships must still be supervised by pro heroes.
antiLore: Do not let tournament success make students legally independent heroes.

entries.title: Todoroki Does Not Become Fully Social Overnight

coordinates.label: Deku uses damaging One For All bursts

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shoto’s breakthrough softens his isolation but does not instantly make him socially fluent or emotionally open.
content.injection: Post-festival Shoto can be more willing to connect, but he should remain reserved and awkward while processing family trauma.
antiLore: Do not turn him into a cheerful extrovert immediately.
